key_insight: The paper proves optimal-order H1 error estimates for first- and second-order A-stable backward difference full discretizations of Willmore flow on closed surfaces.
engineering_relevance: It gives rigorous convergence guarantees for high-order evolving-surface FEM methods used to simulate curvature-driven surface evolution with controlled accuracy.
method: Evolve a coupled second-order surface PDE system with finite elements of degree at least two in space.
Apply backward difference time stepping of order 1 or 2 with A-stability.
Use energy estimates plus Dahlquist G-stability and Nevanlinna-Odeh multiplier techniques for the proof.
evidence_signal: The abstract states that numerical experiments are used to illustrate and complement the theoretical error estimates.
watch_item: The analysis assumes polynomial degree at least two and relies on a specialized stability framework, so implementation must preserve the coupled antisymmetric structure carefully.
paper_title: Error estimates for $A$-stable backward difference full discretizations of Willmore flow of closed surfaces
